item.title: Everything is AI
item.description: A Nightmare on LLM Street

item.title: Trump scraps planned signing of US housing affordability bill
item.description: Bipartisan legislation is designed to boost supply and curb institutional investors’ role in property market

item.title: Train in fatal UK crash passed red signal, investigators say
item.description: Brakes were applied nine seconds before the impact but it slowed only from 75mph to 49mph

item.title: Berlin warship U-turn hits defence sector as KNDS heads to market
item.description: Slump in Rheinmetall shares comes on same day tankmaker fires starting gun on long-awaited IPO

item.title: UK banks defeat watchdog in legal battle over historic loan complaints
item.description: High Court rules that Financial Ombudsman Service unlawfully allowed consumers to challenge terms beyond 6-year limit

item.title: Stripe and Anthropic invest in combating respiratory diseases
item.description: Consortium puts $500mn into finding new drugs and scaling up air-treatment technologies

item.title: China detains two Japanese nationals over alleged rare earth smuggling
item.description: Escalation of dispute with Tokyo comes as Beijing opens whistleblower hotline for violations of critical mineral exports

item.title: Energy Source Forum: How to power data centres
item.description: China and the US are the clear frontrunners in data centre rollouts as they battle for AI leadership

item.title: How AI is transforming natural disaster prediction
item.description: Catastrophe scientists are pushing past the limits of physics-based models, improving how insurers calculate risk

item.title: What happened to Starmer’s ‘clean energy superpower’ plan?
item.description: Outgoing UK leader was hesitant to argue the case for his government’s ambitious energy goals

item.title: Britain’s grid operator calls for more electricity as temperatures soar
item.description: Extreme heat increases demand for power and reduces efficiency of solar panels

item.title: Systemic maternity failures led to hundreds of deaths and injuries, review finds
item.description: Damning report into Nottingham NHS trust criticises culture of bullying and staff ignoring pregnant women

item.title: Dassault wins EU legal claim that private jets can be environmentally sustainable
item.description: European Court of Justice rules against Brussels’ exclusion of business jets from new green rules

item.title: EU set to clear Paramount’s $111bn takeover of Warner Bros
item.description: David Ellison’s media group is discussing remedies with Brussels in order to clear key regulatory hurdle

item.title: SK Hynix bets on AI demand with bumper $29bn US listing
item.description: South Korean chipmaker capitalises on central role in global AI boom

item.title: FirstFT: Venezuela to initiate world’s largest sovereign restructuring
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter, Hegseth forces out top US army officer in Europe and Colombian president-elect’s inspirations

item.title: The Indian entrepreneur who will lead WhatsApp
item.description: Kunal Shah has experience in building transaction platforms and insights into emerging markets

item.title: Hyundai workers in South Korea vote to strike over fears of robots replacing them
item.description: Union at country’s largest carmaker wants greater say over how AI and automation are introduced

item.title: Nationalising Thames Water can be easier than it looks
item.description: The UK’s next PM faces some hard decisions. Bringing the failing utility into state ownership isn’t one of them

item.title: Tankmaker KNDS starts process for listing in Paris and Frankfurt
item.description: IPO will test investor appetite for defence stocks and comes as the region embarks on a rearmament drive

item.title: Segro rejects £12.6bn takeover bid from US rival Prologis
item.description: UK landlord’s shares jump 18 per cent as real estate group urges Segro shareholders to pressure company’s board to engage

item.title: SpaceX stumbles
item.description: Ordinary IPO behaviour

item.title: The scoop on the largest restructuring in history
item.description: Venezuela is set to reveal a $240bn debt pile, much higher than previously thought

item.title: Struggling European carmakers have options, none of them good
item.description: Forging alliances with China would help the struggling sector offload some of its bloated costs. It may be the pick of a bad bunch

item.title: Qatar says US-Iran hotline essential to reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Prime Minister Sheikh Mohammed says Gulf state will resume normal LNG production within weeks

item.title: BT’s pension fund lost £300mn on Thames Water
item.description: One of UK’s biggest private schemes wrote off stake in beleaguered utility in 2024

item.title: The red flags at HSBC over funds linked to Lebanese central bank scandal
item.description: The lender’s Swiss unit is under formal investigation over whether it helped Riad Salame embezzle funds

item.title: How a $20bn bet paid off for Africa’s richest man
item.description: Critics dub Aliko Dangote a crony capitalist. He says he is pioneering Asian-style economic development

item.title: Private equity’s move into youth sports brings some truths home
item.description: Investments create benefits but also lead to higher costs and risk of inequality

item.title: US defence groups to meet Trump as missile production struggles
item.description: The administration is seeking to rebuild conventional weapons stockpiles while regearing for modern warfare
